rationale: Chest pain in patient with ischaemic heart disease → possible ACS.
Chest pain in known heart failure → high risk cardiac decompensation / ACS.
Undifferentiated chest pain without cardiac history → urgent same-day assessment.
Acute SOB in COPD → possible exacerbation / respiratory failure.
SOB in asthma → risk of severe attack / need for nebulisers.
New SOB without chronic lung disease → needs urgent clinical review.
Acute confusion in diabetes → consider hypo/hyperglycaemia or infection.
Focal neurological deficit → possible stroke / TIA.
Suicidal ideation → emergency mental health assessment.
Known anxiety with acute deterioration → urgent but not usually life-threatening.
Fever in CKD → higher risk of sepsis / AKI, needs urgent review.
Simple fever without significant comorbidity → likely self-limiting infection.
Uncomplicated sore throat → self-care with safety-netting.
Mechanical back pain without red flags → routine review.
